turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#e8f6ff"/>
  <!-- cheerful face -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="160" fill="#ffd54a" stroke="#f0a500" stroke-width="6" />

  <!-- squinting laughing eyes -->
  <path d="M190 220 q30 -36 60 0" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" />
  <path d="M262 220 q30 -36 60 0" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" />

  <!-- tears of joy -->
  <path d="M170 240 c-14 26 -14 44 0 70 c14 -26 14 -44 0 -70" fill="#88d8ff" stroke="#5fb0e6" stroke-width="2" />
  <path d="M342 240 c-14 26 -14 44 0 70 c14 -26 14 -44 0 -70" fill="#88d8ff" stroke="#5fb0e6" stroke-width="2" />

  <!-- nose -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="270" r="18" fill="#ff6b6b" stroke="#e04b4b" stroke-width="3" />

  <!-- mouth (open laughing mouth) -->
  <path d="M170 310 q86 110 172 0 q-86 45 -172 0 Z" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  <!-- teeth / upper grin -->
  <path d="M190 300 q66 -30 132 0 v12 q-66 18 -132 0 Z" fill="#ffffff" />
  <!-- tongue -->
  <path d="M240 320 q16 26 32 0 q-16 20 -32 0 Z" fill="#ff8aa2" />

  <!-- subtle laugh lines at cheeks -->
  <path d="M150 270 q20 10 30 6" fill="none" stroke="#d88f00" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M362 270 q-20 10 -30 6" fill="none" stroke="#d88f00" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#e8f6ff"/>
  <!-- cheerful face -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="160" fill="#ffd54a" stroke="#f0a500" stroke-width="6" />

  <!-- squinting laughing eyes -->
  <path d="M190 220 q30 -36 60 0" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" />
  <path d="M262 220 q30 -36 60 0" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" />

  <!-- tears of joy -->
  <path d="M170 240 c-14 26 -14 44 0 70 c14 -26 14 -44 0 -70" fill="#88d8ff" stroke="#5fb0e6" stroke-width="2" />
  <path d="M342 240 c-14 26 -14 44 0 70 c14 -26 14 -44 0 -70" fill="#88d8ff" stroke="#5fb0e6" stroke-width="2" />

  <!-- nose -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="270" r="18" fill="#ff6b6b" stroke="#e04b4b" stroke-width="3" />

  <!-- mouth (open laughing mouth) -->
  <path d="M170 310 q86 110 172 0 q-86 45 -172 0 Z" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  <!-- teeth / upper grin -->
  <path d="M190 300 q66 -30 132 0 v12 q-66 18 -132 0 Z" fill="#ffffff" />
  <!-- tongue -->
  <path d="M240 320 q16 26 32 0 q-16 20 -32 0 Z" fill="#ff8aa2" />

  <!-- subtle laugh lines at cheeks -->
  <path d="M150 270 q20 10 30 6" fill="none" stroke="#d88f00" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M362 270 q-20 10 -30 6" fill="none" stroke="#d88f00" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Designing a funny SVG**

I need to create an SVG that visually represents "funny" without using any text or letters. To achieve this, I might draw clues like a humorous clown, a laughing face, or symbols like giggles and smiles. A good idea could be a smiling face with a big grin and squinted eyes, maybe a clown nose and a jester hat. I should also think of combining elements like a party horn and classic theater masks for added clarity. I'll aim to use bright colors and simple shapes.**Creating the SVG for "funny"**

I need to make sure the SVG doesn’t use any letters or numbers. I'll start by setting a light blue background, then create a big yellow circular face in the center. For the eyes, I’ll use upward curved lines to indicate a laughing squint, and a wide open black mouth with white teeth to emphasize the laughter. I could add blue teardrops for "tears of joy" and maybe a party hat or jester elements. I'll use simple vector shapes and ensure proper SVG dimensions, returning the SVG markup in JSON format.**Formatting the SVG in JSON**

I need to return valid JSON containing the SVG markup, and I'm thinking through the details carefully. I must include quotes around the string and properly escape any internal quotes in the SVG using backslashes. The developer's instruction is clear: I should return just the JSON object with a single field named "svg." 

So, my final output will be a correctly formatted string in JSON that adheres to these guidelines. This requires some attention to ensure it’s done right!**Creating the SVG markup**

I’m working on constructing the SVG markup, starting with the main structure. The background will be a light blue rectangle, and I’ll include a large yellow circle for the face. Since I can't use any letters or numbers, I’ll focus on drawing: the eyes will be curved arcs, and I'll use paths with a black stroke for squinting. I’ll make a big open mouth, a small red clown nose, and blue teardrops for tears of joy. After crafting the SVG, I’ll ensure to escape quotes for the JSON format.**Refining the SVG design**

I'm crafting the SVG markup, starting with the overall structure. The background will be a light color, and I’ll add a yellow circular face in the center. For the squinting eyes, I'll use curved lines as paths, and I'll incorporate teardrop shapes for tears of joy. The nose will be a small red circle, and I plan to create a big black mouth resembling an arc with white teeth. I need to ensure everything looks good, carefully handle escape quotes for the JSON string, and finalize the SVG.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
